steps: Tank capacity = 1200 litres. Daily usage = 150 litres. Need to find first day when remaining < 200 litres. Let remaining after n days = 1200 - 150n. Solve 1200 - 150n < 200 → 1200 - 200 < 150n → 1000 < 150n → n > 1000/150 = 6.666... So n must be at least 7 full days. Check remaining after 6 days: 1200 - 150×6 = 1200 - 900 = 300 litres (still ≥ 200). After 7 days: 1200 - 150×7 = 1200 - 1050 = 150 litres (< 200). Starting Monday morning full, after Monday (day 1) → Tuesday morning? Actually careful: 'starts completely full on Monday morning and no new water is added'. We want the day when it first has less than 200 litres. After each full day of usage, we check at the end of the day (or next morning before usage?). The wording 'on which day will the tank first have less than 200 litres remaining?' likely means at the end of that day. Let's compute end-of-day remaining: Monday end: 1200 - 150 = 1050. Tuesday end: 1050 - 150 = 900. Wednesday end: 750. Thursday end: 600. Friday end: 450. Saturday end: 300. Sunday end: 150. So at the end of Sunday, remaining = 150 < 200. That means the tank first has less than 200 litres remaining on Sunday. But check options: Sunday is option C. However, careful: 'on which day' could mean the day when you notice it has less than 200 litres, which would be at the start of that day? If we check at the start of each day: Monday start: 1200. Tuesday start: 1050. Wednesday start: 900. Thursday start: 750. Friday start: 600. Saturday start: 450. Sunday start: 300. Monday (next week) start: 150. So at the start of Monday (next week) it is <200. That would be option D. But the problem likely means after usage that day. Let's re-read: 'If the tank starts completely full on Monday morning and no new water is added, on which day will the tank first have less than 200 litres remaining?' Typically such problems consider the remaining after the day's usage. At the end of Saturday, remaining = 300, still above 200. At the end of Sunday, remaining = 150, below 200. So the first day it falls below 200 is Sunday. But wait: Could it be during the day? They use 150 litres per day, so it decreases gradually. It will drop below 200 sometime during the last day. But they ask 'on which day' — likely the whole day when it first ends below 200. That is Sunday. However, let's check the options: Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday (next week), Thursday. If we think it's Sunday, that's C. But maybe they mean at the beginning of the day: At the beginning of Sunday, it's 300, still above 200. At the beginning of Monday (next week), it's 150, below 200. So that would be Monday (next week) = D. Which is correct? Let's see the exact wording: 'on which day will the tank first have less than 200 litres remaining?' It doesn't specify 'at the start' or 'at the end'. In many such problems, they consider after the day's usage. But let's test with a simpler example: If tank holds 300 litres, usage 150 per day, starts full Monday morning. When does it first have less than 200? After Monday usage: 150 < 200, so Monday. That would be intuitive. So by analogy: After Monday usage: 1050, not <200. After Tuesday: 900, not <200. ... After Saturday: 300, not <200. After Sunday: 150 <200. So Sunday. That seems consistent. So answer should be Sunday = C. But wait, check the calculation: 1200 - 150×6 = 300 at end of Saturday. 1200 - 150×7 = 150 at end of Sunday. So indeed Sunday. However, some might interpret 'day' as the day when you first notice it below 200 at the start of the day. That would be Monday next week. But the problem says 'starts completely full on Monday morning' and 'no new water is added', so we check each morning? Usually these problems ask: 'On which morning will the tank first have less than 200 litres?' But here it says 'on which day will the tank first have less than 200 litres remaining?' Possibly ambiguous. Let's see the options: both Sunday and Monday are there. Which one is mathematically correct? If we consider the remaining at the end of each day, it's Sunday. If we consider at the start of each day, it's Monday. The phrase 'first have less than 200 litres remaining' could be interpreted as the first point in time when the amount is less than 200. That happens during the day on Sunday, because at the start of Sunday it's 300, after using some water it will drop below 200. So the day when it first goes below 200 is Sunday. So I think C is correct. But let's verify: They use 150 L per day, so usage rate = 150/24 ≈ 6.25 L/h. Starting Sunday morning with 300 L, to drop to 200 L requires using 100 L, which takes 100/6.25 = 16 hours. So around late Sunday evening it drops below 200. So indeed during Sunday. Therefore the day is Sunday. So answer is C. However, many 11+ questions expect you to compute full days: 1200 - 150n < 200 → n > 6.67, so after 7 days of usage, which means end of Sunday. So I'll go with C. But wait, the options: A) Friday, B) Saturday, C) Sunday, D) Monday (next week), E) Thursday. My calculation says Sunday. But let's double-check: 150×7 = 1050 used, leaving 150. That is after 7 days of usage. Monday is day 1, Tuesday day 2, ..., Sunday is day 7. So at the end of day 7 (Sunday), remaining = 150. So answer is Sunday. Therefore I choose C.
